assertions.description: Spec has all required sections including Acceptance Criteria
No code fences outside Implementation Notes
assertions.check: Count ``` outside Implementation Notes. Even one = FAIL.

assertions.description: No TypeScript types in Design Decisions
assertions.check: Design Decisions must not contain 'interface ', 'type ', ': string', ': number', ': boolean', '=> Promise' or other TS syntax

assertions.description: No JSON/proto shapes in Design Decisions
assertions.check: Design Decisions must not contain JSON-like shapes or data structure definitions.

assertions.description: Scope items are capabilities not file paths
assertions.check: In Scope must not contain 'src/features/...', 'NEW: src/...', or file extensions like '.ts', '.tsx'.

assertions.description: Bold topic sentences in Design Decisions
assertions.check: Do >80% of Design Decisions paragraphs open with a bold sentence stating a decision as fact?
Could a competent developer start implementing? Spec must name patterns, state management, tool behaviors, and state coordination.

assertions.description: Design Decisions are decisions not implementation
assertions.check: FAIL if Design Decisions contains code fences, TS interfaces, JSON shapes, exact line numbers, mechanism tables, or React hook implementation details.
Are >50% of failure modes genuine policy decisions the team could disagree on?
Are >80% of In Scope items behavioral capabilities rather than file/component names?
## Acceptance Criteria exists with 4-8 behavioral checkbox items. Items must be observable outcomes, NOT implementation tasks. FAIL if missing, <4 items, or items are implementation tasks.
Must have Agent-Verifiable subsection with action → outcome format checks. FAIL if all non-build checks are labeled Manual/Human-Only.

assertions.description: Spec has an Appetite field in the header
assertions.check: The spec header must contain an '**Appetite:**' field with a value like 'Small Batch (~1-2 weeks)' or 'Full Cycle (~6 weeks)' or similar time budget. FAIL if the field is missing entirely or contains only a placeholder like '[...]'.

assertions.description: Design Decisions use three-tier boundaries where appropriate
assertions.check: Scan Design Decisions for three-tier implementation boundaries (Always/Ask First/Never or equivalent like Must/Should/Must Not). The spec should contain at least 2 boundary statements across the three tiers — e.g., 'destructive tools always require confirmation' (Always tier) and 'never add a new Zustand store' (Never tier). The boundaries give the implementer a decision model for gray areas. FAIL if zero boundary-tier language is present. PASS if at least 2 distinct tier statements exist, even if not explicitly labeled 'Always/Ask First/Never'.
